summary: P0 Bug Fix VERIFIED: AI Risk Panel in Command Center and Operator Dashboard now working correctly. Backend fix: Added user_id field (from senior.guardian_id) to incident API responses. Frontend: DispatchPanel correctly uses user_id for Guardian AI API calls. All 13 backend tests passed. Frontend testing confirmed both AI panels display risk scores, levels, factors, and recommendations.

TestOperatorIncidentsUserIdField.test_incidents_include_user_id_field: PASS - Each incident has user_id field (mapped from senior.guardian_id)
TestOperatorIncidentsUserIdField.test_incidents_have_expected_fields: PASS - Incidents have all required fields: id, senior_id, user_id, device_id, incident_type, severity, status, created_at, senior_name

TestCommandCenterActiveIncidents.test_command_center_incidents_have_user_: PASS - Active incidents include user_id field

TestGuardianAIHighRiskEndpoint.test_high_risk_user_has_required_fields: PASS - Each user has: user_name, final_score, risk_level, top_factors, recommended_action

TestGuardianAIRiskScoreEndpoint.test_risk_score_response_structure: PASS - Response has: final_score, risk_level, top_factors, action_detail

TestIntegrationFlow.test_incident_user_id_can_fetch_risk_sco: PASS - user_id from incidents works with Guardian AI risk-score API

command_center.page_loads: PASS - Command Center page loads with data-testid='command-center-page'
command_center.ai_risk_intelligence_panel: PASS - AI Risk Intelligence panel found (data-testid='ai-risk-intelligence')
command_center.top_factors_shown: PASS - 'Activity outside normal hours', 'Alert frequency above normal' visible

operator_dashboard.page_loads: PASS - Operator Dashboard loads with data-testid='operator-dashboard'
operator_dashboard.dispatch_panel: PASS - DispatchPanel displays when incident selected
operator_dashboard.ai_risk_panel: PASS - AI Risk Panel shown in DispatchPanel (tested with TEST_Senior_5a216fe5)
operator_dashboard.why_flagged_section: PASS - Shows: Activity outside normal hours, Alert frequency above normal, User in high-risk geographic zone
operator_dashboard.recommended_section: PASS - Shows: Increase monitoring frequency and alert guardian

critical_code_review_comments: Backend operator.py Lines 49-85: GET /api/operator/incidents correctly joins Senior table and extracts guardian_id as user_id
Backend operator.py Lines 3614-3642: GET /api/operator/command-center active_incidents also includes user_id
Frontend OperatorDashboard.jsx Lines 160-167: DispatchPanel correctly uses incident.user_id || incident.senior_id for AI API call
Frontend CommandCenterPage.jsx Lines 126, 157: Fetches /guardian-ai/insights/high-risk and displays high-risk users
guardian_ai_v2.py: All endpoints properly return required fields for frontend display
seed_data_creation: Used existing incidents and seniors data
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 Bug Fix Complete: The AI Risk Panel in Command Center and Operator Dashboard now works correctly. Root cause was incidents having senior_id but Guardian AI API needing user_id. Fix: Backend now maps senior.guardian_id to user_id in incident responses. Frontend DispatchPanel uses incident.user_id for AI risk-score calls. Command Center's AIRiskIntelligence component fetches high-risk users from /guardian-ai/insights/high-risk which already worked. Both panels display: risk score (0-10), risk level (low/moderate/high/critical), top factors, and recommended actions.
verified_features: GET /api/guardian-ai/insights/high-risk returns high_risk_users with scores and factors
Command Center AI Risk Intelligence panel displays high-risk users with scores
Operator Dashboard DispatchPanel shows AI Risk Panel when incident selected
AI Risk Panel shows: score, risk level badge, 'Why flagged' factors, 'Recommended' action
rca_of_the_issue: Root Cause: Incidents stored senior_id (FK to seniors table) but Guardian AI API required user_id (FK to users table). The mapping is: seniors.guardian_id = users.id. Fix: Modified /api/operator/incidents and /api/operator/command-center to JOIN seniors table and include guardian_id as user_id in response. Frontend DispatchPanel was updated to use incident.user_id || incident.senior_id for backward compatibility.
